title: Queen's Gambit Declined: Main Line
summary: Learn the solid Queen's Gambit Declined, where Black declines the gambit and fights for equality through central control.
description: The QGD is one of the most respected defenses to 1.d4. You'll learn why Black's acceptance of a slightly worse but solid position is sound, and how White tries to squeeze a small advantage from the symmetrical setup.
objectives: Understand the symmetrical pawn structure and why it favors White slightly.
Learn the typical plans for both sides in the QGD.
Master the bishop pair advantage in closed positions.
plans: White uses the bishop pair and central control to maintain a small but lasting edge.
Black seeks to simplify and trade pieces, reducing White's attacking chances.
The key is patience: White should improve pieces while Black waits for a concrete plan.
commonMistakes: Do not rush. The advantage is small, so accurate play is needed.
Avoid trading pieces prematurely. The bishop pair is valuable in this structure.
Do not underestimate Black's solidity. With accurate play, Black can equalize.
completionTitle: White has a slight but lasting edge
completionSummary: The QGD is a positional masterpiece where White's patient play is rewarded with a small but persistent advantage.

steps.title: Control the center from White's side
steps.instruction: Play d4 to claim central space and open lines for your pieces.
steps.coaching: The queen's pawn opening is solid and classical, controlling key central squares.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black matches in the center
steps.instruction: Black responds with d5, establishing a symmetrical central structure.
steps.coaching: The Queen's Gambit position begins. Black challenges for the center.

steps.title: The Queen's Gambit
steps.instruction: Offer the c-pawn to disrupt Black's central control.
steps.coaching: This is the Queen's Gambit: White offers a pawn to gain better development and central control.
steps.hint: Push the c-pawn to challenge d4.

steps.title: Black declines the gambit
steps.instruction: Black protects the d5 pawn with e6, maintaining central control.
steps.coaching: This is the Queen's Gambit Declined. Black chooses solidity over material.

steps.title: Develop the knight to the center
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center and supporting the d4 pawn.
steps.coaching: Natural development. The knight controls d5 and b5, and supports the center.
steps.hint: Bring the queen's knight to c3.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight moves to f6, attacking e4 and supporting central control.
steps.coaching: Black develops soundly. The knight also prepares castling.

steps.title: Take the pawn
steps.instruction: Capture on d5, gaining a pawn while opening lines.
steps.coaching: White accepts the pawn. Now Black must decide how to recapture.
steps.hint: Capture on d5 with the pawn.

steps.title: Black recaptures
steps.instruction: Black takes back with the pawn, opening the e-file.
steps.coaching: The position is now balanced. White has a small central advantage.

steps.title: Develop the bishop with pressure
steps.instruction: Move Bg5, pressuring f6 and developing actively.
steps.coaching: This is a standard move in the QGD, pinning the knight and developing the bishop.
steps.hint: Develop the bishop to g5, targeting the knight on f6.
